notes: Pattern reuse from evolution.ts (seeded harmonics, curve cache, PRNG) is well-identified and appropriate
Integration point in renderLoop.ts is correctly identified: after evolveParams (line 143) and before renderer.render (line 200)

Bass energy usage note: smoothBass in renderLoop is raw 0-255 range, while params.bassEnergy is normalized 0-1 via bassToMacro(). Plan says updateCamera receives smoothBass but step 2 says params.bassEnergy — should use the normalized bassEnergy for consistency
Base camera position (0, 0, 5) matches scene.ts line 49 exactly
motionAmplitude is a pass-through in evolution.ts (line 142), so it correctly reflects reduced-motion preference each frame
Pointer isolation is confirmed — pointer only feeds pointerDisturbance in mappings.ts, never touches camera

issues.description: Minor inconsistency: step 1 exports say updateCamera takes bassEnergy, but step 2 integration says pass smoothBass. Use params.bassEnergy (normalized 0-1) for cleaner API — the 0.15 multiplier in the plan assumes normalized input.
issues.suggestion: Use params.bassEnergy (normalized 0-1) consistently in both the API signature and the call site

issues.description: No camera.lookAt() is set in scene.ts initial setup — camera defaults to looking down -Z. The plan's drifting lookAt target will work but should ensure the initial lookAt(0,0,0) is set explicitly before the first updateCamera call to avoid a visible jump.
issues.suggestion: Set camera.lookAt(0, 0, 0) in initCameraMotion or on the first updateCamera call

estimated_complexity_agreement: agree — single new file plus ~5 lines in renderLoop.ts, reusing proven patterns
recommendation: Implement as planned with the two minor fixes noted above
